Container.Name: FridgeShare
Container.Overview: A virtual fridge door for your household. Put sticky notes, letter magnets, photos, emoji, and drawings on a shared board. Give your household the edit link, or share a read-only link for everyone else. No accounts. No tracking. Your data stays on your server.
Container.Description: FridgeShare is a self-hosted virtual fridge board for households and small groups.[br][br] Put sticky notes, letter magnets, photos, emoji, and drawings on a shared board. Give your household the edit URL (which includes the edit token as a URL fragment), or strip the token for a read-only link.[br][br] [b]Edit access:[/b] The edit token lives in the URL fragment; it is never sent to the server in page loads. Share the full URL to give edit access; share without the fragment for view-only.[br][br] [b]Admin token:[/b] Set SELFHOST_ADMIN_TOKEN before exposing FridgeShare outside your LAN. This restricts board creation and deletion to people who have the token. Regular board editing still uses the per-board edit token.[br][br] [b]Backup:[/b] Everything is in the data directory. Back it up with a simple file copy.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the FridgeShare web interface. Open http://[IP]:[PORT]/ after install.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Where boards, uploads, and directory metadata are stored. Back this directory up before upgrading.

Config.Name: Admin Token
Config.Description: Recommended before exposing outside your LAN. Required to create or delete boards. If left blank, a token is auto-generated on first start and saved to appdata/fridgeshare/.admin-token.

Config.Name: Port (env)
Config.Description: Must match the port mapping above.

Config.Name: Trust Proxy
Config.Description: Set to 1 if FridgeShare is behind a reverse proxy (e.g. Nginx Proxy Manager) that sets X-Forwarded-For. Leave blank for direct access.
